title: parallax cross-model review round (verifier output)
description: What the independent verifier emits for ONE review round of a slice. It is the single, unified shape the verifier authors — so the ledger can be built mechanically by scripts/merge-ledger.py without the orchestrating Claude inventing spec_refs, evidence, ids or lifecycle (which Parallax forbids: the producer must not author its own certification). `findings` are the problems the verifier currently sees; `resolved` are prior findings it has POSITIVELY re-verified as now satisfying the spec (absence alone never resolves a finding). Used at post_green and for sole-mode RED arbitration; pre-freeze uses spec-adversary.schema.json.

verdict.description: pass = nothing blocking this round (findings MUST be empty); concerns = at least one finding.

findings.description: Problems the verifier sees in the CURRENT assembled tree. Each must be spec-grounded with concrete evidence.

id.description: OPTIONAL — when re-reporting a finding you were handed, echo its exact ledger id (e.g. S2-N3) so merge-ledger binds to it precisely instead of guessing by fingerprint. Omit for a genuinely new finding.

spec_ref.description: The spec section/anchor violated — REQUIRED (no spec_ref => inadmissible style opinion).

where.description: Location file[:line]; the file part is the stable fingerprint key.

functional_repro.description: True if a reproducible functional error (always blocks regardless of severity).

resolved.description: Prior findings the verifier RE-CHECKED and confirms now satisfy the spec against the current tree. merge-ledger.py marks the matching ledger finding fixed + verified_by=codex. Only the verifier may populate this — it is the positive confirmation that a fix held. Cite `id` (echoed from the prior findings handed to you) so the resolution is unambiguous even when two defects share a kind|spec_ref|file; without an id, merge-ledger falls back to the first still-live finding of that fingerprint.

id.description: OPTIONAL but PREFERRED — the exact ledger finding id being resolved (e.g. S2-N3), echoed from the prior findings you were given. Disambiguates colliding fingerprints. merge-ledger matches by this id first, then by fingerprint.
